Class Piece

java.lang.Object
core.Piece
All Implemented Interfaces:
Moveable
Direct Known Subclasses:
Archer, Archmage, Assassin, Dragon, Mage, Warrior
public abstract class Piece extends Object implements Moveable
An abstract representation of a game piece. Provides base attributes such as color and position, as well as common utility methods for determining movement rules and board interactions.

  • Field Details

    • color

      protected Color color

    • position

      protected Position position

    • hasMoved

      protected boolean hasMoved

  • Constructor Details

    • Piece

      public Piece(Color color, Position position)
      Initializes a new piece with a specific color and starting position. By default, a newly created piece is marked as not having moved.
      Parameters:
      color - the color of the piece
      position - the initial position on the board

  • Method Details

    • getColor

      public Color getColor()
      Retrieves the color of this piece.
      Returns:
      the piece's color

    • getPosition

      public Position getPosition()
      Retrieves the current position of this piece on the board.
      Returns:
      the piece's position

    • hasMoved

      public boolean hasMoved()
      Checks whether this piece has moved from its initial starting position.
      Returns:
      true if the piece has moved, false otherwise

    • setPosition

      public void setPosition(Position position)
      Updates the piece's position and marks it as having moved.
      Parameters:
      position - the new position to assign to the piece

    • canMoveTo

      protected boolean canMoveTo(Position pos, Board board)
      Determines if the piece can legally move to the specified position. A move is generally considered valid if the target position is within bounds and is either empty or occupied by an enemy piece.
      Parameters:
      pos - the target position to check
      board - the current game board
      Returns:
      true if the position is a valid destination, false otherwise

    • isEnemy

      protected boolean isEnemy(Position pos, Board board)
      Checks if a given position is occupied by an enemy piece.
      Parameters:
      pos - the position to check
      board - the current game board
      Returns:
      true if an enemy piece occupies the position, false otherwise

    • isEmpty

      protected boolean isEmpty(Position pos, Board board)
      Checks if a given position on the board is currently empty.
      Parameters:
      pos - the position to check
      board - the current game board
      Returns:
      true if the position is within bounds and unoccupied

    • slide

      protected List<Position> slide(int dr, int dc, Board board)
      Calculates a list of valid moves by sliding continuously in a specific direction. The slide stops when it hits an invalid position, a friendly piece, or after capturing an enemy piece.
      Parameters:
      dr - the row direction offset (e.g., -1 for up, 1 for down)
      dc - the column direction offset (e.g., -1 for left, 1 for right)
      board - the current game board
      Returns:
      a list of valid positions along the sliding path

    • resetHasMoved

      public void resetHasMoved(boolean value)
      Manually overrides the movement status of the piece. Useful for undoing moves or specific game resets.
      Parameters:
      value - the boolean value to set the hasMoved flag to

    • getLegalMoves

      public abstract List<Position> getLegalMoves(Board board)
      Description copied from interface: Moveable
      Calculates all valid destination positions for this entity based on the current board state.
      Specified by:
      getLegalMoves in interface Moveable
      Parameters:
      board - the current state of the game board
      Returns:
      a list of legal positions this entity can move to

    • getSymbol

      public abstract String getSymbol()
      Description copied from interface: Moveable
      Retrieves the visual symbol or string representing this entity.
      Specified by:
      getSymbol in interface Moveable
      Returns:
      the symbol representing the entity

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the piece for debugging purposes.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string containing the color, class name, and current position
